instruction: Under what circumstances would a person be affected by Section 8 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: On ceasing to hold office, the Chairperson and every Member shall be ineligible for— (i) reappointment as the Chairperson or a Member of the Lokpal; (ii) any diplomatic assignment, appointment as administrator of a Union territory and such other assignment or appointment which is required by law to be made by the President by warrant under his hand and seal; (iii) further employment to any other office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of a State; (iv) contesting any election of President or Vice-President or Member of either House of Parliament or Member of either House of a State Legislature or Municipality or Panchayat within a period of five years from the date of relinquishing the post.
output: A practical application of this section arises when on ceasing to hold office, the Chairperson and every Member shall be ineligible for— (i) reappointment as the Chairperson or a Member of the Lokpal; (ii) any diplomatic assignment, appointment as administrator of a Union territory and such other assignment or appointment which is required by law to be made by the President by warrant under his hand and seal; (iii) further employment to any other office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of a State; (iv) contesting any election of President or Vice-President or Member of either House of Parliament or Member of either House of a State Legislature or Municipality or Panchayat within a period of five years from the date of relinquishing the post.

instruction: What legal obligations or rights does subsection (1) of Section 47 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) impose?
output: Where any offence under sub-section (1) of section 46 has been committed by any society or association of persons or trust (whether registered or not), every person who, at the time the offence was committed, was directly in charge of, and was responsible to, the society or association of persons or trust, for the conduct of the business or affairs or activities of the society or association of persons or trust as well as such society or association of persons or trust shall be deemed to be guilty of the offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly: Provided that nothing contained in this sub-section shall render any such person liable to any punishment provided in this Act, if he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he had exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of such offence.

instruction: What is the legal interpretation of subsection (2) under Section 8 of THE RELIGIOUS INSTITUTIONS (PREVENTION OF MISUSE) ACT, 1988 (1988)?
input: Where any manager or other employee of a religious institution is accused of an offence under this Act and a charge-sheet for the prosecution of such person is filed in any court and the court is of the opinion, after considering the charge-sheet and after hearing the prosecution and the accused, that a prima facie case exists, it shall pass an order or direction restraining the person from exercising the powers or discharging the duties of his office or post pending trial.
output: This provision mandates that where any manager or other employee of a religious institution is accused of an offence under this Act and a charge-sheet for the prosecution of such person is filed in any court and the court is of the opinion, after considering the charge-sheet and after hearing the prosecution and the accused, that a prima facie case exists, it must pass an order or direction restraining the person from exercising the powers or discharging the duties of his office or post pending trial.

instruction: What is the legal interpretation of subsection (3) under Section 42 of THE CO-OPERATIVE SOCIETIES ACT, 1912 (1912)?
input: Subject to any rules, a liquidator appointed under this section shall, in so far as such powers are necessary for carrying out the purposes of this section, have power to summon and enforce the attendance of witnesses and to compel the production of documents by the same means and (so far as may be) in the same manner as is provided in the case of a Civil Court under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (5 of 1908).

instruction: What is covered under Section 39 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
output: If any public servant is convicted of an offence under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 (49 of 1988) by the special Court, notwithstanding and without prejudice to any law for the time being in force, it may make an assessment of loss, if any, caused to the public exchequer on account of the actions or decisions of such public servant not taken in good faith and for which he stands convicted, and may order recovery of such loss, if possible or quantifiable, from such public servant so convicted: Provided that if the Special Court, for reasons to be recorded in writing, comes to the conclusion that the loss caused was pursuant to a conspiracy with the beneficiary or beneficiaries of actions or decisions of the public servant so convicted, then such loss may, if assessed and quantifiable under this section, also be recovered from such beneficiary or beneficiaries proportionately.

instruction: What rights, duties, or liabilities arise from subsection (1) of Section 30 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: The Lokpal, when it provisionally attaches any property under sub-section (1) of section 29 shall, within a period of thirty days of such attachment, direct its Prosecution Wing to file an application stating the facts of such attachment before the Special Court and make a prayer for confirmation of attachment of the property till completion of the proceedings against the public servant in the Special Court.
output: This provision mandates that the Lokpal, when it provisionally attaches any property under sub-section (1) of section 29 must, within a period of thirty days of such attachment, direct its Prosecution Wing to file an application stating the facts of such attachment before the Special Court and make a prayer for confirmation of attachment of the property till completion of the proceedings against the public servant in the Special Court.

instruction: In plain language, what does subsection (3) of Section 4 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) mean?

output: This provision mandates that the Selection Committee must for the purposes of selecting the Chairperson and Members of the Lokpal and for preparing a panel of persons to be considered for appointment as such, constitute a Search Committee consisting of at least seven persons of standing and having special knowledge and expertise in the matters relating to anti-corruption policy, public administration, vigilance, policy making, finance including insurance and banking, law and management or in any other matter which, in the opinion of the Selection Committee, can be useful in making the selection of the Chairperson and Members of the Lokpal: however not less than fifty per cent. of the members of the Search Committee must be from amongst the persons belonging to the Scheduled Castes, the Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes, Minorities and women: Provided further that the Selection Committee can also consider any person other than the persons recommended by the Search Committee.

instruction: What rights, duties, or liabilities arise from subsection (3) of Section 17 of THE BIHAR AND WEST BENGAL (TRANSFER OF TERRITORIES) ACT, 1956 (1956)?
input: Notwithstanding anything contained in sub-sections (1) and (2), but save as hereinafter provided, the High Court at Patna shall have, and the High Court at Calcutta shall not have, jurisdiction to entertain, hear or dispose of appeals, applications for leave to appeal to the Supreme Court, applications for review and other proceedings, where any such proceedings seek any relief in respect of any order passed by the High Court at Patna before the appointed day: Provided that if, after such proceedings have been entertained by the High Court at Patna, it appears to the Chief Justice of that High Court that they ought to be transferred to the High Court at Calcutta, he shall order that they shall be so transferred and such proceedings shall thereupon be transferred accordingly.
output: This provision mandates that despite other provisions anything contained in sub-sections (1) and (2), but save as hereinafter provided, the High Court at Patna must have, and the High Court at Calcutta must not have, jurisdiction to entertain, hear or dispose of appeals, applications for leave to appeal to the Supreme Court, applications for review and other proceedings, where any such proceedings seek any relief in respect of any order passed by the High Court at Patna before the appointed day: however if, after such proceedings have been entertained by the High Court at Patna, it appears to the Chief Justice of that High Court that they ought to be transferred to the High Court at Calcutta, he must order that they must be so transferred and such proceedings must thereupon be transferred accordingly.

output: This provision mandates that the public debt of Bihar attributable to loans taken from the Central Government, the Reserve Bank of India or any other bank before the appointed day must be divided between Bihar and West Bengal in proportion to the total expenditure on all capital works and other capital outlays incurred up to the appointed day in the territories of Bihar excluding the transferred territories and in the transferred territories, respectively: however for the purposes of such division, only expenditure on assets for which capital accounts have been kept must be taken into account.

output: This provision mandates that if any company contravenes the provisions of sub-section (1), then, without prejudice to any other proceedings which can be taken against the company, every director, manager, secretary and any other officer of that who is knowingly a party to such contravention must be punishable with fine which must not be less than two lakh rupees but which can extend on first conviction to ten lakh rupees, and on any subsequent conviction with fine which must not be less than four lakh rupees but which can extend to twenty lakh rupees.

instruction: What is the legal interpretation of subsection (4) under Section 30 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: If the public servant is subsequently convicted of the charges of corruption, the proceeds relatable to the offence under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 (49 of 1988) shall be confiscated and vest in the Central Government free from any encumbrance or leasehold interest excluding any debt due to any bank or financial institution. Explanation.—For the purposes of this sub-section, the expressions "bank", "debt" and "financial institution" shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in clauses (d), (g) and (h) of section 2 of the Recovery of Debts Due to Banks and Financial Institutions Act, 1993 (51 of 1993).
output: This provision mandates that if the public servant is subsequently convicted of the charges of corruption, the proceeds relatable to the offence under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 (49 of 1988) must be confiscated and vest in the Central Government free from any encumbrance or leasehold interest excluding any debt due to any bank or financial institution. Explanation.—For the purposes of this sub-section, the expressions "bank", "debt" and "financial institution" must have the meanings respectively assigned to them in clauses (d), (g) and (h) of section 2 of the Recovery of Debts Due to Banks and Financial Institutions Act, 1993 (51 of 1993).

instruction: Give a concise legal overview of subsection (5) under Section 7C of THE BANARAS HINDU UNIVERSITY ACT, 1915 (1915).
input: If, in the opinion of the Vice-Chancellor, any emergency has arisen which requires immediate action to be taken, the Vice-Chancellor shall take such action as he deems necessary and shall report the same for approval at the next meeting to the authority which, in the ordinary course, would have dealt with the matter: Provided that, if the action taken by the Vice-Chancellor is not approved by the authority concerned, he may refer the matter to the Visitor, whose decision thereon shall be final. Provided further that, where any such action taken by the Vice-Chancellor affects any person in the service of the University, such person, shall be entitled to prefer, within thirty days from the date on which he receives notice of such action, an appeal to the Executive Council.
output: If, in the opinion of the Vice-Chancellor, any emergency has arisen which requires immediate action to be taken, the Vice-Chancellor shall take such action as he deems necessary and shall report the same for approval at the next meeting to the authority which, in the ordinary course, would have dealt with the matter: Provided that, if the action taken by the Vice-Chancellor is not approved by the authority concerned, he may refer the matter to the Visitor, whose decision thereon shall be final.

instruction: Elaborate on the statutory scope of subsection (5) of Section 27 of THE TEZPUR UNIVERSITY ACT, 1993 (1993).

output: This provision mandates that despite other provisions anything contained in the foregoing sub-sections, the Visitor can make new or additional Statutes or amend or repeal the Statutes referred to in sub-section (1), during the period of three years immediately after the commencement of this Act: however the Visitor can, on the expiry of the said period of three years, make, within one year from the date of such expiry, such detailed Statutes as he can consider necessary and such detailed Statutes must be laid before both Houses of Parliament.

instruction: Provide a scenario where subsection (1) of Section 29 of THE CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS ACT, 1949 (1949) becomes relevant.

output: This provision mandates that the Visitor can, if the inspection or inquiry is made in respect of the University or any College or Institution maintained by it, address the Vice-Chancellor with reference to the result of such inspection or inquiry together with such views and advice with regard to the action to be taken thereon, as the Visitor can be pleased to offer, and on receipt of address made by the Visitor, the Vice-Chancellor must communicate to the Board of Management the views of the Visitor with such advice as the Visitor can offer upon the action to be taken thereon.

instruction: How should subsection (2) of Section 23 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) be construed under law?

instruction: What does the legislature intend by subsection (2) of Section 17 of THE BRAHMAPUTRA BOARD ACT, 1980 (1980)?

instruction: What rights, duties, or liabilities arise from subsection (1) of Section 32 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: Where the Lokpal, while making a preliminary inquiry into allegations of corruption, is prima facie satisfied, on the basis of evidence available,— (i) that the continuance of the public servant referred to in clause (d) or clause (e) or clause (f) of sub-section (1) of section 14 in his post while conducting the preliminary inquiry is likely to affect such preliminary inquiry adversely; or (ii) such public servant is likely to destroy or in any way tamper with the evidence or influence witnesses, then, the Lokpal may recommend to the Central Government for transfer or suspension of such public servant from the post held by him till such period as may be specified in the order.
output: This provision permits that where the Lokpal, while making a preliminary inquiry into allegations of corruption, is prima facie satisfied, on the basis of evidence available,— (i) that the continuance of the public servant referred to in clause (d) or clause (e) or clause (f) of sub-section (1) of section 14 in his post while conducting the preliminary inquiry is likely to affect such preliminary inquiry adversely; or (ii) such public servant is likely to destroy or in any way tamper with the evidence or influence witnesses, then, the Lokpal can recommend to the Central Government for transfer or suspension of such public servant from the post held by him till such period as can be specified in the order.

instruction: How does subsection (1) of Section 15 of THE BRAHMAPUTRA BOARD ACT, 1980 (1980) read?

instruction: Summarize the statutory mandate of subsection (3) of Section 12 of THE TEZPUR UNIVERSITY ACT, 1993 (1993).
input: The Vice-Chancellor may, if he is of opinion that immediate action is necessary on any matter, exercise any power conferred on any authority of the University by or under this Act and shall report to such authority the action taken by him on such matter: Provided that if the authority concerned is of opinion that such action ought not to have been taken, it may refer the matter to the Visitor whose decision thereon shall be final: Provided further that any person in the service of the University who is aggrieved by the action taken by the Vice-Chancellor under this sub-section shall have the right to appeal against such action to the Board of Management within three months from the date on which decision on such action is communicated to him and thereupon the Board of Management may confirm, modify or reverse the action taken by the Vice-Chancellor.

instruction: Under what circumstances would a person be affected by Section 32 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: Where the Lokpal, while making a preliminary inquiry into allegations of corruption, is prima facie satisfied, on the basis of evidence available,— (i) that the continuance of the public servant referred to in clause (d) or clause (e) or clause (f) of sub-section (1) of section 14 in his post while conducting the preliminary inquiry is likely to affect such preliminary inquiry adversely; or (ii) such public servant is likely to destroy or in any way tamper with the evidence or influence witnesses, then, the Lokpal may recommend to the Central Government for transfer or suspension of such public servant from the post held by him till such period as may be specified in the order.
output: A practical application of this section arises when where the Lokpal, while making a preliminary inquiry into allegations of corruption, is prima facie satisfied, on the basis of evidence available,— (i) that the continuance of the public servant referred to in clause (d) or clause (e) or clause (f) of sub-section (1) of section 14 in his post while conducting the preliminary inquiry is likely to affect such preliminary inquiry adversely; or (ii) such public servant is likely to destroy or in any way tamper with the evidence or influence witnesses, then, the Lokpal may recommend to the Central Government for transfer or suspension of such public servant from the post held by him till such period as may be specified in the order.

instruction: Elaborate on the statutory scope of subsection (2) of Section 9 of THE EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT, 1950 (1950).
input: Every rule made under this Act shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which may be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions aforesaid, both Houses agree in making any modification in the rule or both Houses agree that the rule should not be made, the rule shall thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case may be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment shall be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that rule.
output: This provision mandates that every rule made under this Act must be laid, as soon as can be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which can be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions mentioned above, both Houses agree in making any modification in the rule or both Houses agree that the rule should not be made, the rule must thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case can be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment must be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that rule.

instruction: Provide a legal summary of subsection (1) of Section 47 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014).

output: This provision mandates that the Lokpal must, despite other provisions anything contained in section 4 of the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946 (25 of 1946) and section 8 of the Central Vigilance Commission Act, 2003 (45 of 2003), have the powers of superintendence over, and to give direction to the Delhi Special Police Establishment in respect of the matters referred by the Lokpal for preliminary inquiry or investigation to the Delhi Special Police Establishment under this Act: however while exercising powers of superintendence or giving direction under this sub-section, the Lokpal must not exercise powers in such a manner so as to require any agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) to whom the investigation has been given, to investigate and dispose of any case in a particular manner.

instruction: What rights, duties, or liabilities arise from subsection (5) of Section 7C of THE BANARAS HINDU UNIVERSITY ACT, 1915 (1915)?

Provided further that, where any such action taken by the Vice-Chancellor affects any person in the service of the University, such person, shall be entitled to prefer, within thirty days from the date on which he receives notice of such action, an appeal to the Executive Council.
output: This provision mandates that if, in the opinion of the Vice-Chancellor, any emergency has arisen which requires immediate action to be taken, the Vice-Chancellor must take such action as he deems necessary and must report the same for approval at the next meeting to the authority which, in the ordinary course, would have dealt with the matter: however, if the action taken by the Vice-Chancellor is not approved by the authority concerned, he can refer the matter to the Visitor, whose decision thereon must be final. instruction: How should subsection (2) of Section 26 of THE CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS ACT, 1949 (1949) be construed under law?

instruction: Provide a legal explanation of subsection (2) as defined in Section 44 of THE TEZPUR UNIVERSITY ACT, 1993 (1993).
input: Every Statute, Ordinance or Regulation made under this Act shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which may be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions aforesaid, both Houses agree in making any modification in the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation or both Houses agree that the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation should not be made, the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation shall thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case may be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment shall be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that Statute, Ordinance or Regulation.
output: This provision mandates that every Statute, Ordinance or Regulation made under this Act must be laid, as soon as can be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which can be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions mentioned above, both Houses agree in making any modification in the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation or both Houses agree that the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation should not be made, the Statute, Ordinance or Regulation must thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case can be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment must be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that Statute, Ordinance or Regulation.

instruction: How should subsection (3) of Section 42 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) be construed under law?

instruction: What is the legal interpretation of subsection (5) under Section 20 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: In case the Lokpal decides to proceed to investigate into the complaint, it shall direct any agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) to carry out the investigation as expeditiously as possible and complete the investigation within a period of six months from the date of its order: Provided that the Lokpal may extend the said period by a further period not exceeding of six months at a time for the reasons to be recorded in writing.
output: This provision mandates that in case the Lokpal decides to proceed to investigate into the complaint, it must direct any agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) to carry out the investigation as expeditiously as possible and complete the investigation within a period of six months from the date of its order: however the Lokpal can extend the said period by a further period not exceeding of six months at a time for the reasons to be recorded in writing.

instruction: In plain language, what does subsection (1) of Section 12 of THE BRAHMAPUTRA BOARD ACT, 1980 (1980) mean?

output: This provision mandates that subject to the other provisions of this Act and the rules, the Board must carry out surveys and investigations in the Brahmaputra Valley and prepare a Master Plan for the control of floods and bank erosion and improvement of drainage in the Brahmaputra Valley: however the Board can prepare the Master Plan in parts with reference to different areas of the Brahmaputra Valley or with reference to different matters relating to such areas and can, as often as it considers necessary so to do, revise the Master Plan or any part of that.

instruction: What is the legal interpretation of subsection (1) under Section 26 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014)?
input: If the Lokpal has reason to believe that any document which, in its opinion, shall be useful for, or relevant to, any investigation under this Act, are secreted in any place, it may authorise any agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) to whom the investigation has been given to search for and to seize such documents.

instruction: Explain the legal significance of subsection (2) of Section 47 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014).
input: Notwithstanding anything contained in sub-section (1), where an offence under this Act has been committed by a society or association of persons or trust (whether registered or not) and it is proved that the offence has been committed with the consent or connivance of, or is attributable to any neglect on the part of, any director, manager, secretary or other officer of such society or association of persons or trust, such director, manager, secretary or other officer shall also be deemed to be guilty of that offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.
output: This provision mandates that despite other provisions anything contained in sub-section (1), where an offence under this Act has been committed by a society or association of persons or trust (whether registered or not) and it is proved that the offence has been committed with the consent or connivance of, or is attributable to any neglect on the part of, any director, manager, secretary or other officer of such society or association of persons or trust, such director, manager, secretary or other officer must also be deemed to be guilty of that offence and must be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.

instruction: How should subsection (1) of Section 47 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) be construed under law?
input: Where any offence under sub-section (1) of section 46 has been committed by any society or association of persons or trust (whether registered or not), every person who, at the time the offence was committed, was directly in charge of, and was responsible to, the society or association of persons or trust, for the conduct of the business or affairs or activities of the society or association of persons or trust as well as such society or association of persons or trust shall be deemed to be guilty of the offence and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly: Provided that nothing contained in this sub-section shall render any such person liable to any punishment provided in this Act, if he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he had exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of such offence.
output: This provision mandates that where any offence under sub-section (1) of section 46 has been committed by any society or association of persons or trust (whether registered or not), every person who, at the time the offence was committed, was directly in charge of, and was responsible to, the society or association of persons or trust, for the conduct of the business or affairs or activities of the society or association of persons or trust as well as such society or association of persons or trust must be deemed to be guilty of the offence and must be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly: however nothing contained in this sub-section must render any such person liable to any punishment provided in this Act, if he proves that the offence was committed without his knowledge or that he had exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of such offence.

instruction: Explain Section 44 of THE BIHAR AND WEST BENGAL (TRANSFER OF TERRITORIES) ACT, 1956 (1956).
output: For the purpose of facilitating the application of any law in relation to Bihar or West Bengal, the appropriate Government may, before the expiration of one year from the appointed day, by order make such adaptations and modifications of the law, whether by way of repeal or amendment, as may be necessary or expedient, and thereupon every such law shall have effect subject to the adaptations and modifications so made until altered, repealed or amended by a competent authority or competent legislature. Explanation.—In this section, the expression "appropriate Government" means as respects any law relating to a matter enumerated in the Union List the Central Government, and as respects any other law, the State Government.

instruction: In plain language, what does subsection (1) of Section 40 of THE BIHAR AND WEST BENGAL (TRANSFER OF TERRITORIES) ACT, 1956 (1956) mean?

output: This provision mandates that despite other provisions anything contained in section 63 of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1939 (4 of 1939), a permit granted by the State Transport Authority of Bihar or by any Regional Transport Authority in Bihar must, if such permit was immediately before the appointed day valid and effective in any area within the transferred territories, be deemed to continue to be valid and effective in that area after that day subject to the provisions of that Act as for the time being in force in that area;

instruction: Summarize the preamble of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014).
output: An Act to provide for the establishment of a body of Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States to inquire into allegations of corruption against certain public functionaries and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. WHEREAS the Constitution of India established a Democratic Republic to ensure justice for all; AND WHEREAS India has ratified the United Nations Convention Against Corruption; AND WHEREAS the Government's commitment to clean and responsive governance has to be reflected in effective bodies to contain and punish acts of corruption; NOW, THEREFORE, it is expedient to enact a law, for more effective implementation of the said Convention and to provide for prompt and fair investigation and prosecution in cases of Corruption.

instruction: What does subsection (6) of Section 20 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) provide?
output: Notwithstanding anything contained in section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 (2 of 1974), any agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) shall, in respect of cases referred to it by the Lokpal, submit the investigation report under that section to the court having jurisdiction and forward a copy thereof to the Lokpal.

instruction: What does subsection (8) of Section 20 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) provide?
output: The Lokpal may, after taking a decision under sub-section (7) on the filing of the charge-sheet, direct its Prosecution Wing or any investigating agency (including the Delhi Special Police Establishment) to initiate prosecution in the Special Court in respect of the cases investigated by the agency.

instruction: Give a practical example of how Section 11 of THE LOKPAL AND LOKAYUKTAS ACT, 2013 (2014) would apply.

output: In practice, this provision applies when notwithstanding anything contained in any law for the time being in force, the Lokpal shall constitute an Inquiry Wing headed by the Director of Inquiry for the purpose of conducting preliminary inquiry into any offence alleged to have been committed by a public servant punishable under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 (49 of 1988): Provided that till such time the Inquiry Wing is constituted by the Lokpal, the Central Government shall make available such number of officers and other staff from its Ministries or Departments, as may be required by the Lokpal, for conducting preliminary inquiries under this Act.

instruction: In plain language, what does subsection (5) of Section 18 of THE CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS ACT, 1949 (1949) mean?

input: If the Lokpal is satisfied that any document seized under sub-section (1) may be used as evidence for the purpose of any investigation under this Act and that it shall be necessary to retain the document in its custody or in the custody of such officer as may be authorised, it may so retain or direct such authorised officer to retain such document till the completion of such investigation: Provided that where any document is required to be returned, the Lokpal or the authorised officer may return the same after retaining copies of such document duly authenticated.
output: This provision mandates that if the Lokpal is satisfied that any document seized under sub-section (1) can be used as evidence for the purpose of any investigation under this Act and that it must be necessary to retain the document in its custody or in the custody of such officer as can be authorised, it can so retain or direct such authorised officer to retain such document till the completion of such investigation: however where any document is required to be returned, the Lokpal or the authorised officer can return the same after retaining copies of such document duly authenticated.

instruction: How should subsection (2) of Section 52 of THE BIHAR AND WEST BENGAL (TRANSFER OF TERRITORIES) ACT, 1956 (1956) be construed under law?
input: Every rule made under this section shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which may be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions aforesaid, both Houses agree in making any modification in the rule, or both Houses agree that the rule should not be made, the rule shall thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case may be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment shall be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that rule.
output: This provision mandates that every rule made under this section must be laid, as soon as can be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, while it is in session, for a total period of thirty days which can be comprised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive sessions mentioned above, both Houses agree in making any modification in the rule, or both Houses agree that the rule should not be made, the rule must thereafter have effect only in such modified form or be of no effect, as the case can be; so, however, that any such modification or annulment must be without prejudice to the validity of anything previously done under that rule.

instruction: Give a practical example of how Section 6 of THE BANKERS' BOOKS EVIDENCE ACT, 1891 would apply.
